title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US special forces conducted confirmed ground operations inside Iranian territory in early April 2026 to rescue downed pilots from F-15 jets shot down during airstrikes, marking the first deliberate US military entries amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict that erupted with joint US-Israeli strikes on February 28. These raids, part of Operation Epic Fury, followed weeks of aerial bombardments targeting Iranian military sites, oil infrastructure like Kharg Island, and nuclear facilities, alongside Iranian counterattacks on US bases. Trader consensus reflects these actions as qualifying under market criteria for terrestrial entry by special operations forces. A two-week ceasefire was agreed on April 7, with negotiations set to begin in Islamabad on April 11, potentially de-escalating further ground risks before the April 30 deadline, though Trump administration statements signal continued pressure on Tehran.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced a conditional two-week ceasefire with Iran on April 7, 2026, just ahead of his self-imposed deadline, mediated by Pakistan and aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz for global shipping while paving the way for broader negotiations. This de-escalation follows intense US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian targets since late February, amid escalating proxy conflicts and threats of wider war. Iran has agreed to the pause, with in-person talks potentially starting April 10 in Islamabad based on a 10-point Iranian plan countering earlier US proposals. Israel supports the truce but excludes Lebanon operations, highlighting fragility as compliance, sanctions relief, and regional security guarantees remain key sticking points for traders assessing longer-term diplomatic prospects.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain leads trader consensus for the 2026 FIFA World Cup at 16.0% implied probability, buoyed by a dominant UEFA qualifying campaign capped by a 3-0 friendly win over Serbia last week, showcasing their young, fluid attack featuring Yamal and Pedri. France (13.8%) trails closely after surging to the top of recent power rankings with consistent wins, while England (11.3%) maintains steady form despite a 1-1 draw against Uruguay. Defending champions Argentina (9.2%) and Brazil (8.6%) remain competitive via secure CONMEBOL spots, but the bunched top reflects the expanded 48-team field's depth, finalized qualifiers excluding Italy, balanced group draws, and three months until the June kickoff allowing shifts from injuries or Nations League results.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

markets.question: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by March 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: US, Israel, and Iran agreed to a tentative two-week ceasefire on April 8, 2026, halting airstrikes and missile exchanges while reopening the Strait of Hormuz for shipping, averting President Trump's threatened escalation against Iranian power plants and infrastructure. This follows over five weeks of intense hostilities since February 28, including US-Israeli degradation of Iran's missile capabilities and retaliatory barrages killing dozens, amid rejected prior proposals. Negotiations begin Friday in Islamabad via Pakistani mediation, testing de-escalation amid proxy risks from Hezbollah and Houthis. Traders gauge holdout potential against historical breakdowns, with UN Security Council discussions underscoring diplomatic pressures.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads Polymarket trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Masters, buoyed by his world No. 1 ranking, two prior green jackets, and elite ball-striking suited to Augusta National's demands, despite withdrawing from last week's Houston Open for his newborn's arrival—now with family support on-site. The race stays tightly bunched with Jon Rahm (7.9%) surging via top-five LIV finishes this year, Bryson DeChambeau (6.5%) leveraging power off the tee, defending champion Rory McIlroy (6.5%) chasing history post-Grand Slam, and Xander Schauffele (5.8%) boasting five top-10s here, amid a thinned field after Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson withdrawals. Benign weather—mostly sunny, low-30s mph winds Thursday—favors scoring, heightening upset potential in this no-dominant-favorite scenario.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the Democratic presidential nominee frontrunner at 26.5% implied probability, reflecting his executive experience, fundraising prowess, and sustained national profile from prior anti-Trump advocacy, even as recent March 2026 polls like Echelon Insights show Kamala Harris leading nationally at 21% amid a fragmented field. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.3%, buoyed by progressive enthusiasm and youth appeal in surveys, while Jon Ossoff's 5.9% highlights Georgia swing-state viability. Diverging from some polling averages, markets emphasize electability post-2024 loss; consolidation may hinge on 2026 midterm results, early fundraising disclosures, key endorsements, or debates over party direction in primaries starting late 2027.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil futures plummeted over 14% on April 8, 2026, to around $96 per barrel after reports of a U.S.-Iran agreement eased escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, unwinding a risk premium that had propelled prices above $117 earlier in the week amid President Trump's deadline pressures. This sharp reversal reflects trader consensus pricing in reduced geopolitical disruptions, compounded by EIA data showing U.S. crude inventories climbing to near three-year highs and OPEC+ signaling modest output increases despite tight global supply dynamics. Weaker demand outlooks from China and subdued U.S. economic growth forecasts further cap upside potential, with market-implied probabilities favoring sub-$100 bins for April resolution. Key catalysts ahead include Thursday's EIA weekly storage report and ongoing Hormuz monitoring.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus at 18% implied probability for the 2028 presidential election winner, reflecting his strong position as heir apparent in Republican primary polling—dominating at 46-53% in recent HarrisX and JL Partners surveys—bolstered by incumbency advantages and alignment with President Trump's America First agenda. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16%, buoyed by his rising profile amid a fragmented Democratic field post-Harris's weak 41% primary standing and his criticisms of Trump policies on tariffs and immigration. Marco Rubio's 10% share stems from a mid-March surge tied to effective diplomacy as Secretary of State, though odds have stabilized. The tight race persists due to Trump's two-term limit opening the field, with 2026 midterms in swing states poised to award early momentum, potential endorsements, or scandals capable of widening gaps ahead of primaries.

title: Rockets vs. Suns
description:

eventMetadata.context_description: With Kevin Durant facing his former Suns squad for the first time since his blockbuster summer trade to Houston, trader consensus reflects the Rockets' strong positioning as the No. 5 seed in the Western Conference at 48-29 entering this late-season clash, bolstered by a five-game win streak including blowouts over the Jazz and Bucks plus a narrow victory at Golden State. Phoenix, clinging to the No. 7 play-in spot at 42-35, relies on home-court advantage at Footprint Center after snapping a skid with a road win over Chicago, though Houston holds a perfect 3-0 head-to-head edge this season. Both teams enter relatively healthy—Suns missing only Haywood Highsmith (knee), Rockets without Steven Adams—heightening focus on seeding implications with playoffs looming.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2028 Republican presidential nomination shows a tight race among Robert F. Kennedy Jr. (49%), Vice President J.D. Vance (39%), and Senator Marco Rubio (21%), driven by uncertainty over President Trump's endorsement as the party's kingmaker amid his two-term limit. Recent reports of Trump privately polling major donors, with most favoring Rubio over Vance, have boosted the senator's odds and narrowed Vance's VP incumbency edge. Kennedy Jr.'s HHS role and Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) push attract populist voters seeking food and pharma reforms, though resistance from Big Ag lobbies tempers gains. 2026 midterms and administration milestones could tip the balance in this early primary field.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 62-16 record and status as defending 2025 champions, bolstered by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play, anchor their 40.5% implied probability as traders' clear consensus favorite entering the playoffs with the West's top seed locked. San Antonio Spurs (60-19) trail closely at 14.8% following their franchise-record start and key trade deadline acquisition of De'Aaron Fox to pair with Victor Wembanyama, whose recent rib injury X-rays came back negative. Boston Celtics' steady 54-25 Eastern Conference contention and Denver Nuggets' nine-game win streak support their shares, while Detroit Pistons' surprise East lead at 57-22 elevates them amid a wide-open postseason field.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders, wagering real capital, price a 98.5% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting strong consensus after the March 17-18 policy session held the fed funds target at 3.50%-3.75% amid stable inflation near 2.4% YoY in February CPI and the dot plot signaling just one cut later in 2026. The March nonfarm payrolls report, released April 3, added 178,000 jobs with unemployment steady at 4.3%, exceeding expectations and reinforcing a balanced labor market that sidelines near-term easing or hikes. This positioning aligns with CME FedWatch odds near 98%, though upside inflation surprises in the imminent March CPI or geopolitical oil shocks could prompt a reassessment.

title: Bucks vs. Nets
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Bucks win, the market will resolve to "Bucks".
If the Nets win, the market will resolve to "Nets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Taurean Prince: Assists O/U 2.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Taurean Prince records more than 2.5 assists during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Taurean Prince records 2.5 assists or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

eventMetadata.context_description: Milwaukee Bucks host the Brooklyn Nets on April 10 at Fiserv Forum, with both teams hampered by lengthy injury reports that dominated trader focus after Brooklyn's 96-90 upset victory on Tuesday, powered by E.J. Liddell's career-high 21 points. Bucks remain without Giannis Antetokounmpo (left knee hyperextension and bone bruise), Bobby Portis (left wrist sprain), Gary Trent Jr. (left hip pointer), Kyle Kuzma (right Achilles tendonopathy), and others, thinning their frontcourt and scoring punch late in the regular season. Nets counter missing Nic Claxton (right finger sprain), Noah Clowney (left ankle), Ziaire Williams (left foot), Terance Mann (Achilles/knee), and more, testing depth in a matchup with potential playoff positioning or lottery implications. Home-court advantage and revenge motive could favor Milwaukee, but uncertainty around returns looms large.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Bulls enter Thursday's rematch at Capital One Arena with strong trader consensus at 67% implied probability to defeat the Washington Wizards again, fueled by Tuesday's dominant 129-98 road blowout that snapped a seven-game skid—their first win post-front-office shake-up. Rob Dillingham's career-high 26 points highlighted Chicago's offensive depth despite absences like Josh Giddey (hamstring) and Matas Buzelis (illness). Wizards, holders of the NBA-worst 17-61 record, remain severely depleted by injuries to Trae Young (quadriceps), Anthony Davis (finger), Alex Sarr (illness), D'Angelo Russell, and others, extending their six-game slide. Short rest and Washington's home-court edge offer upset potential, but Bulls' momentum and superior roster health drive the pricing.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US airstrikes hit military targets on Iran's Kharg Island oil export hub—handling 90% of its crude—overnight on April 7, sparing oil infrastructure, as Vice President JD Vance affirmed no strategic shift ahead of President Trump's Strait of Hormuz reopening deadline. This escalates prior March raids amid US-Iran conflict, where Trump has threatened seizure for leverage, though no ground invasion occurred and Iranian reports confirm the island remains under armed forces control with uninterrupted exports. Traders weigh de-escalation signals like a prospective two-week ceasefire against retaliation risks, with upcoming diplomatic talks or further military actions as key catalysts for any control shift.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 8?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' W17 holds a commanding early-season edge in the 2026 F1 drivers' championship, powering George Russell and rookie teammate Kimi Antonelli to the top of the standings after three Grands Prix, with Antonelli leading at 72 points to Russell's 63 following his back-to-back wins in China and Japan. Trader consensus implies a 44.5% probability for Russell slightly ahead of Antonelli's 30.8%, reflecting the Briton's experience and Australia victory despite recent bad luck like a battery failure and untimely safety car in Suzuka that dropped him to fourth. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc trails at 49 points after consistent podiums, but Mercedes' 45-point constructors' lead underscores their pace advantage heading into the mid-April break, with team principal Toto Wolff greenlighting an intra-team title fight sans contact.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves head into their matchup against the Pacers hampered by key absences on the official injury report, with Anthony Edwards out for right knee injury maintenance and Jaden McDaniels sidelined by left knee patella tendinopathy, forcing reliance on depth pieces like Julius Randle, Rudy Gobert, Mike Conley, and Ayo Dosunmu. The Pacers face a far graver roster crisis, missing Tyrese Haliburton (Achilles tear), Pascal Siakam (ankle sprain), Andrew Nembhard (thoracic/lumbar management), T.J. McConnell (hamstring), Aaron Nesmith (cervical strain), and others including Johnny Furphy (ACL tear), rendering their 18-60 Eastern Conference bottom-feeders even more vulnerable at Gainbridge Fieldhouse. Minnesota, holding sixth in the West at 46-32, aims to solidify a direct playoff spot amid a recent three-game skid and injury pileup, while Indiana plays out the string with no postseason stakes.

This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final High price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT High prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certainty that the Iranian regime will endure past April 30, driven by its effective suppression of dissent amid the ongoing US-Israel airstrikes and economic strain. Recent pro-regime rallies—over 850 since the war's start, including in late March—have bolstered cohesion, while brutal crackdowns, including April 4 executions of dissident group members, have quelled unrest following January's crushed nationwide protests. Intelligence assessments from Israeli officials and US sources confirm no imminent collapse, with the IRGC maintaining control despite leadership losses and infrastructure hits. Scenarios like mass elite defections, Supreme Leader Khamenei's incapacitation, or a sudden popular uprising could shift odds, though structural barriers and short timeline to resolution limit plausibility.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's announcement on April 7 of a two-week suspension of bombing and attacks on Iran—conditional on Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz—signals de-escalation in the U.S.-Israeli military campaign initiated February 28, 2026, driving trader consensus toward a formal end-of-operations declaration in coming months. Recent reports indicate core objectives like neutralizing nuclear capabilities are near completion, with U.S. officials warning of depleted targets after weeks of airstrikes. Negotiations begin Friday in Islamabad, potentially resolving Hormuz access amid Iran's prior rejections of ceasefire proposals. The fragile truce, expiring around April 22, and ongoing hostilities highlight risks of renewed escalation before any conclusive announcement.

title: Atlanta Braves vs. Los Angeles Angels
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Atlanta Braves and Los Angeles Angels, scheduled for April 7 at 9:38PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Atlanta Braves" if the Atlanta Braves win the game.

This market will resolve to "Los Angeles Angels" if the Los Angeles Angels win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "No Run" if no runs are scored in the 1st inning by either team.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlanta Braves right-hander Grant Holmes takes the mound against Los Angeles Angels lefty Reid Detmers in the rubber match of their interleague series at Angel Stadium, following a 1-1 split. The Angels edged the Braves 6-2 on Monday behind José Soriano's gem over Chris Sale, but Atlanta rebounded 7-2 Tuesday with Ozzie Albies' third homer fueling a potent offense amid a benches-clearing brawl sparked by Jorge Soler and Reynaldo López. Early-season records hover near .500 for both clubs (Braves 7-5, Angels 6-6), hampered by injuries: Atlanta without Spencer Strider (oblique strain, injured list) and Sean Murphy (hip, out until late April), Angels missing relievers like Kirby Yates (knee). Holmes dazzled with six scoreless frames last start versus Arizona, while Detmers boasts a shutout history in six career innings against the Braves, tilting trader consensus toward a pitcher's duel with home-field edge for the Halos.

title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: A recent two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran, announced on April 7 amid ongoing airstrikes and naval operations, has eased immediate escalation risks, anchoring trader consensus at 76.5% against a U.S. ground invasion before 2027. Following U.S.-Israeli airstrikes on February 28 targeting Iranian sites including nuclear facilities and infrastructure, President Trump issued ultimatums over the Strait of Hormuz closure but shifted to diplomacy via Pakistan, with both sides claiming victories. No official ground troop deployments have occurred despite discussions of amphibious plans; military leaders emphasize air and missile dominance over costly occupation amid congressional pushback like the No War Against Iran Act and logistical challenges. Upcoming ceasefire expiration could test negotiations, but historical aversion to Middle East ground wars reinforces de-escalation sentiment.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich's impressive away victory over Real Madrid in the Champions League quarterfinal first leg on April 7 has edged them to a slim 30.5% implied probability lead as trader consensus favorite, leapfrogging Arsenal—who posted a solid 1-0 win at Sporting CP—now at 26.5%, keeping the top duo tightly bunched amid vulnerable aggregate ties. Barcelona (16.5%) and PSG (14.5%) lurk closely after grinding through round-of-16 knockouts, bolstered by strong league-phase table finishes and home second legs next week, while Liverpool, Real Madrid, and Atletico face uphill climbs post-first-leg setbacks. Unpredictable UCL knockouts, favorable paths avoiding early clashes, and no major injury disruptions fuel the competitive dynamics, with wisdom-of-crowds pricing reflecting real-money bets on potential semifinals upsets.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) as third-place finisher in Colombia's Chamber of Representatives election, reflecting official Registraduría Nacional tallies from the March 8, 2026, legislative vote that allocate PLC 26 seats behind Pacto Histórico's 33–42 and Centro Democrático's 24–30. This positioning stems from PLC's consistent 11–12% national vote share and broad departmental presence, outpacing Partido Conservador (18–19 seats) and others like Partido de la U (12 seats) despite a fragmented Congress requiring coalitions for governance. With results certified and no major recounts pending ahead of May presidential polls, shifts would require rare legal challenges or seat reallocations from special circunscripciones, though historical patterns show minimal post-election adjustments.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Finland as the clear frontrunner at 38.1% implied probability for Eurovision 2026 victory, driven by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin," which dominated Finland's UMK national final on February 28 with nearly triple the points of rivals, blending rock, classical, and dance for broad jury-televote appeal. Recent OGAE poll results, including France's 12 points to Finland, surging YouTube views as of April 7, and leading bookmaker odds post-Super Saturday selections have solidified this momentum, despite Semi-Final 1 qualification risk on May 12 in Vienna. France (11.8%) benefits from Big Five direct final entry with Monroe's "Regarde!," while Denmark (10.2%) holds steady via Søren Torpegaard Lund's emotive Danish-language "Før vi går hjem" from Melodi Grand Prix. Semi-final running orders released early April add positioning intrigue ahead of the May 16 grand final.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Hungary's parliamentary election set for April 12, trader consensus favors Péter Magyar at 63.5% implied probability to become next prime minister, reflecting recent polls where his TISZA party leads Viktor Orbán's Fidesz by 23 points (58%-35%) among decided voters per Medián's March survey—the firm's strong track record adds credibility. Magyar, a former Orbán ally turned challenger, has surged on youth turnout (over 65% under-30 support) and urban momentum amid economic discontent after 16 years of Fidesz rule. Orbán retains rural and older voter bases but trails in aggregates; U.S. Vice President JD Vance's recent Budapest visit aimed to bolster him. A TISZA majority in the National Assembly would install Magyar, though Hungary's single-member districts could narrow leads.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 53% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic returning to normal by April 30, reflecting a closely balanced standoff amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire terms announced this week via Pakistani mediation. Iran pledged an "immediate and safe" reopening following February 28 disruptions that slashed transits by over 90%, with recent upticks—15 vessels in 24 hours, the highest since the blockade—driving Yes odds higher but failing to restore pre-crisis volumes of 30,000 annual passages. Persistent risks from AIS spoofing, port fires, elevated insurance premia, and selective exemptions for allies like Iraq sustain skepticism. Key swing factors include US enforcement of its Tuesday deadline and real-time transit data; Brent crude relief rallies hinge on sustained normalization to avert prolonged 12% global oil supply shocks.

title: Trump out as President by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 98.3% for President Trump remaining in office through April 30, driven by steep procedural barriers to removal despite recent partisan noise. Over the Easter weekend, unsubstantiated social media rumors of a Walter Reed hospitalization sparked 25th Amendment calls and health speculation, swiftly denied by the White House amid Trump's April 6 press conference appearance. Fresh impeachment articles filed by Rep. John Larson (D-CT) cite Iran threats, echoing Democratic demands, but lack GOP House support for advancement; Senate conviction requires a two-thirds supermajority improbable with Republican control. VP Vance and Cabinet loyalty blocks Section 4 invocation. Realistic shifts demand unforeseen health crisis, resignation, or accelerated bipartisan revolt—none evident in three-week window.

title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M at 49.5% implied probability to win Colombia's May 31 presidential first round, narrowly ahead of Historic Pact's Iván Cepeda Castro (42.5%) and Centro Democrático's Paloma Valencia (40.4%), reflecting a fragmented race post-March 8 legislative elections where Petro's left secured the most Senate seats but no majority, forcing coalition dynamics. Recent polls like AtlasIntel (March fieldwork) show Cepeda leading first-round intentions at 35-36%, with de la Espriella and Valencia trailing, yet traders discount this amid economic stagnation, rising security concerns, and Cepeda's vulnerability in runoffs—where right-wingers lead hypotheticals. The race stays tight due to undecided voters and moderate consolidation potential for Candidate M; endorsements, debates, or scandals before June 21 runoff could create separation.
